item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Loved it, would double upvote if I could.
My only dislike is the shortness, I wish this article was far longer.

Thank you for the read.

item.title: Re: +1
item.content:encoded: We wanted to give just enough description to leave the reader wanting, so I can say I’m glad you feel that way! :)

"Superman is real!"
At first I thought that was a really dumb premise but you definitely pull it off. I wish we got a little more information on the Man of Steel though.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Ah, yes. "Keeping quiet." The ol' Virtual Amnestic.

item.title: one of the images isn't hosted on site
item.content:encoded: hey, don't know if it really matters, but page 4 is hosted on imgur while the others are hosted on-site.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The leaked log of Kent inspired Superman.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Unfortunately, I'm finding myself a bit lost on what happened here. What did the losing of the ASCI logs have to do with Schuster and Kent? I guess I'm a bit lost.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I agree wholeheartedly and I thank you for your comment. Always makes my day to get a TL333s comment!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is marvelous! The verisimilitude is off the charts. It seemed goofy at first that Superman was created because of an ASCI info leak, but I found the way this plays out charming. This is sadly overlooked!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: this really had me going for a minute. i was rolling my eyes and ready to call this a high-effort attempt at putting superman in the scp universe. im pretty certain this is the intent, what with the seemingly deliberate naming of the characters and all. anyway, i appreciated the subversion even if it took a while to get to it and i definitely appreciate the effort that went into creating these documents. despite that, i feel as if the core anomaly shouldn't be about the leaking of the documents and rather the man of steel himself, with the documents serving as an incident occurring in relation to him. either way, +1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This page has a robust energy and I thoroughly enjoyed both the article and the display of effort that went into creating it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
a more elegant edit as soon as is allowed

If you are worried about the rules of the SCP-5000 contest, don't be. This time around edits, minor and major, are allowed throughout the contest, including the contest's voting period.

And sorry for borking your image links with one of my revisions earlier.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I’ve smudged that, it was an error. We’ll try to do a more elegant edit as soon as is allowed.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Did hard drives exist back in the 1930s?

It's going to be a novote for me. I admire the verisimilitude, but I think that 4128 did "Superman was real" better.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
WE MADE IT.

This SCP would not have been possible without the incredible Caro, who had the idea and did the real leg work of making the documents look good. She deserves far more credit than I do. Also thanks to shaggy for making sure the ending didn't suck, Dexanote for providing Bridge's line, and Tanhony for providing much-needed moral support, as well as Elogee Fishtruck and everybody else who read this in its evolving phases.

The text version is here if you have trouble reading parts of it, use reader software, want to translate or anything else. For any translator trying to recreate the look of the documents: soak in coffee for two minutes, making sure there's an even spread. Sprinkle some coffee grinds, then bake in the oven until the edges of the page curl up. It's time consuming, but boy is it worth it.

Thank you for reading :)

Images were created by:

ASCI header is by viperfishy
Joe Shuster's signiature is wikimedia commons.
All other image work done by Caro and I and scanned at Walgreens.

If we do not achieve the greatest underdog victory in history we would like the earliest available Series VI slot.
